questions: What’s your experience with photography? Have you ever been in a darkroom before?

Read Exodus 2:1-10. In these verses, Moses is rescued by the daughter of the man trying to kill him and then raised in the palace of his people's oppressor. Where do you see the irony here? How does this remind us that God is working behind the scenes even when he seems silent?
Read Exodus 2:11-15. How do these verses reveal the limits of human justice apart from God? How did this failure essentially force Moses into a "darkroom season"?

Read Exodus 2:16-22. Moses was Hebrew by birth, he was raised as an Egyptian in power, and now he’s living as a Midianite shepherd. Why was it necessary for Moses to be developed in private before he was ready to lead in public? What is lost when we try to skip the "private" season of growth?

Read Exodus 2:23-25. Read 1 Peter 2:11. Moses never fully “fits” anywhere. In what ways have you felt like a foreigner in your own life? How can that sense of not "fitting in" actually be a tool God uses to develop us?

Moses spent a total of 80 years being prepared before God called him to lead Israel. Why are we so prone to viewing seasons of waiting as "wasted" time? As you reflect on your own life, can you identify a difficult season of waiting that you can now see was a season of development?

Are there areas in your life right now where you feel like you are in the darkroom? Are there ways that it feels like God is silent? How can Moses’ story change the way you view your current circumstances?

sections.title: Pray
sections.body: Pray that we may grow in understanding how deeply loved we are by God. Pray that we may be drawn into the story of Exodus, and that we may discover more of God’s love on the journey. As we continue to reflect on this message, pray that we would be transformed more into the image of Jesus.

sections.title: Next Steps
sections.body: Check out the resources page for links to several Bible reading plans and other helpful tools. Explore other opportunities for growth and transformation by checking out courses.
